task: Create a brand positioning statement for a B2B analytics platform targeting mid-market companies.
expect: Identifies a specific target audience segment within mid-market (e.g. ops leaders, data teams) rather than staying generic
Articulates clear differentiation from competitors with a specific frame of reference
Follows a structured positioning format (For [target] who [need], [brand] is the [category] that [benefit] unlike [alternative])

task: We're rebranding from a consulting firm to a SaaS product. Develop a transition strategy that retains existing client trust.
expect: Addresses both existing consulting clients and new SaaS prospects as distinct audiences with different concerns
Proposes a phased approach with clear milestones (e.g. soft launch, dual branding period, full cutover)
Includes a messaging bridge that connects the consulting heritage (expertise, relationships) to the SaaS value (scale, self-serve)

task: Two of our product lines have conflicting brand identities — one is premium/enterprise, the other is freemium/SMB. How do we handle this?
expect: Analyses house-of-brands vs branded-house vs endorsed-brand architectures with pros and cons for this scenario
Provides a clear recommendation with specific trade-offs acknowledged (cost of maintaining separate brands vs brand dilution risk)
Includes a naming strategy or naming convention that resolves the conflict
